text: A5 daily overview check: in data/10 life time, the new 2026-05-01 file 02_dotnD_x17p67y20p33_11M_15nshold_3V_med_8psres_9nsdelay_240s.mat currently has 12 populated gate points (n ~ 0 to -0.44 at D = 0.04) and 88 NaN columns. Was this an interrupted/early-save TRPL sweep, or is that partial range the intended dataset?

A5 overnight clarification: in data/10 life time, 07_TLnD_interexc_x21p6y17p6_..._600s.mat looks like an empty template, while 07_TLnD_interexc_x21p6y17p6_..._600s_n0.mat has the first 6/50 D points filled at fixed n=0. Is that the intended new fixed-n D sweep follow-up? Also, do the current TL/BL filenames map to the BL/HTL/LTL regions in the latest A5 deck slide?

A5 daily overview check: the new file data/10 life time/09_spatialmap_aftermov0505_10s_Vt-1_77MH_50nW.mat looks like another after-move remap rather than a new TRPL sweep. In the OpenScience quicklook (/Users/xulab/openscience/lab_assistant/out/daily/2026-05-05_a5_spatialmap_aftermov_compare.png), the brightest region is weaker and shifted lower/left relative to the May 3-4 maps. Should today’s map be summarized as intended recentering on the same candidate spot, or as continued spot-search after losing the earlier bright region?

A5 daily-overview clarification: after the 2026-05-07 fixed-D remap/n-sweep in data/10 life time, the new data/11 lifetime adv branch shows 15_01 TLx23p8y18p5 files at n0Dn0p1 and n0Dn0p08 (11Mtri, 1800 s, 19 delays), then 15_02 TLx23p8y18p5_n0Dn0p1_1144p2_600s_19Mtri_..._6del_30nW.mat, followed by bg_19M/bg_19M2 600/1200/1800 s background files. Should we summarize this as a real follow-up lifetime-advanced measurement branch on the same spot, or mainly as background/setup accumulation before the next interpretable dataset?
